data.content:
I can see the project — an MSTest project targeting .NET 8. The express delivery test expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m`.

I'll start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically whenever you save a file:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has a .NET 8 MSTest project. One test is failing: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects 15.00 but the method returns 10.00 for "express".

They want hot reload set up so they can iterate. I'll use `dotnet watch test` which re-runs tests automatically when files change.

Let me start that up for them.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-476e622254c444a58e92e6433ec63a2a && dotnet watch test --no-restore 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is running and waiting for file changes. It will automatically re-run your tests whenever you save a file.

**Current failing test:** `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expects `15.00m` but `CalculateShipping("express", ...)` returns `10.00m`.

You can now edit `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs` and the tests will re-run automatically. For example, to fix the failing test, change `"express" => 10.00m` to `"express" => 15.00m`.

Use `write_bash` to the `watch` shell or just edit the file — the watcher will pick up changes. When you're done, let me know and I'll stop the watcher.
data.reasoningText:
It seems like the tests ran but dotnet watch didn't show the test output. Let me check - it exited and is waiting for file changes. Let me try running `dotnet watch test` without `--no-restore` to see more output, or maybe I need to look at this differently.

Actually, the output says "Exited" which means the tests ran (and likely failed), but the output might have been truncated. The watcher is now waiting for file changes. This is working correctly - `dotnet watch test` is running and will re-run tests on file changes.

Let me explain this to the user.
